examples.text: mov [BOOT_DRIVE], dl        ; Save boot drive from BIOS
addps  xmm0, xmm2               ; packed sum of the three taps
cld                        ; clear DF - C ABI requires it clear on function entry
mov [BOOT_DRIVE], dl        ; Save boot drive from BIOS
popfd                      ; restore the suspended schedule caller's flags
push ax                     ; Save sector count for later
mov bx, 0x10              ; GDT data selector (4GB limit)
mov cr0, eax              ; PE=1: protected mode (but CS still real-mode)
mov cr0, eax              ; PE=0: back to real mode
mov [BOOT_DRIVE], dl        ; Save boot drive from BIOS
mov cr0, eax              ; PE=1: protected mode (but CS still real-mode)
mov cr0, eax              ; PE=0: back to real mode
mov esp, 0xD00000           ; Boot stack top (2 MB stack from 0xB00000).
addps  xmm0, xmm2               ; packed sum of the three taps
movups xmm3, [third]            ; broadcast 1/3 into all four lanes

capability_priorities.rationale: The shared foundations cross hosted and kernel adapters; CupidDis and hosted CupidASM consume them, while CupidC and the CupidASM kernel adapter remain.
capability_priorities.title: Establish a host-runnable shared Cupid Toolchain core

capability_priorities.rationale: Every compiled C unit and two kernel assembly units cross the ELF32 ET_REL seam.
capability_priorities.title: Emit and consume deterministic ELF32 relocatable objects

capability_priorities.rationale: C code generation, assembly encoding, and disassembly exercise the same 16/32-bit machine domain.
capability_priorities.title: Share one i386 ABI and instruction model

capability_priorities.rationale: Assembler migration needs independent sections, symbols, relocation, and instruction evidence.
capability_priorities.title: Make CupidDis inspect raw and ELF32 relocatable output

capability_priorities.rationale: BITS, ORG, data/reserve forms, times, includes, %define, and label arithmetic gate real sources.
capability_priorities.title: Implement the active Cupid ASM directives and expression language

capability_priorities.rationale: Fixed boot offsets require complete 16/32-bit encoding and ModRM/SIB/address-size behavior.
capability_priorities.title: Reach byte parity for boot and trampoline binaries

capability_priorities.rationale: ISR and context-switch objects must interoperate with host- and CupidC-produced objects.
capability_priorities.title: Emit ELF32 sections, symbols, and i386 relocations

capability_priorities.rationale: Includes, forced headers, conditionals, macro rescanning, paste/stringify, and packing affect every C cohort.
capability_priorities.title: Implement the active C and Cupid C preprocessing contract

capability_priorities.rationale: Kernel and user sources require ILP32 layout, 64-bit arithmetic, callbacks, aggregates, and volatile semantics.
capability_priorities.title: Implement freestanding C11 type, initializer, and cdecl semantics

capability_priorities.rationale: Core platform and tool sources directly depend on attributes, constraints, clobbers, and privileged instructions.
capability_priorities.title: Implement required GNU attributes and extended inline assembly

capability_priorities.rationale: Vendored Doom adds relaxed diagnostics and legacy declaration/callback compatibility without weakening strict C mode.
capability_priorities.title: Compile the complete Doom and compatibility cohort

capability_priorities.rationale: Production-sized globals/includes come before demo-only class, allocation, register, and SIMD extensions.
capability_priorities.title: Scale Cupid mode across embedded programs and browser fragments

source_cohort_order.rationale: Bootstrap the tools that transfer ownership to every later cohort.
Keep the four production transforms CupidASM-owned while retaining NASM only as an optional parity oracle.
Move foundational strict C before vendored compatibility cohorts.
Preserve upstream behavior under a deliberate compatibility mode.
Migrate the remaining separate host-C compilation path to CupidC and stage its CupidLD outputs deliberately.
Keep runtime CupidC/CupidASM regression corpora active through the host migration.
